# Inkwell

> Last updated: 2026-07-17

> Inkwell is a calm, distraction-free writing home for novelists. It combines a
> focused editor with connected world-building tools (characters, lore, and
> story elements), so writers can start a project in seconds and stay
> organized as their story grows. Inkwell's positioning is "the best place to
> actually finish your novel."

Inkwell works in any modern browser, syncs across devices, and supports
offline writing. Documents export as Markdown, plain text, PDF, DOCX, or EPUB.
There is a free plan (Inkling) and a paid plan (Storyteller, $9/month) with a
higher-tier plan (Worldbuilder) coming soon.

Inkwell does not brand its features as "AI." Story intelligence features
(summarization, brainstorming, keeping the story connected) are described by
their outcome, not by the underlying technology.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://inkwell.world/): overview, features, and how to start writing in seconds.
- [Pricing](https://inkwell.world/pricing): plans and what's included in each.
- [App comparisons](https://inkwell.world/compare): how Inkwell compares to other novel-writing apps.
- [Inkwell vs. Scrivener](https://inkwell.world/compare/scrivener)
- [Inkwell vs. Dabble](https://inkwell.world/compare/dabble)
- [Inkwell vs. Ellipsus](https://inkwell.world/compare/ellipsus)
- [Inkwell vs. Google Docs](https://inkwell.world/compare/google-docs)
- [Inkwell vs. Microsoft Word](https://inkwell.world/compare/word)
- [Inkwell vs. Reedsy](https://inkwell.world/compare/reedsy)
- [Best novel writing software 2026](https://inkwell.world/writing-apps-breakdown): a breakdown of eleven novel-writing apps by price, pros, cons, and fit.
- [Privacy Policy](https://inkwell.world/privacy)
- [Terms of Service](https://inkwell.world/terms)

## Getting started

Writers can start using Inkwell immediately at https://app.inkwell.world/auth,
no download or installation required.
